description: Cross-repo governance corpus with explicit dual-source authority model, minimal execution graph, and explicit workflow state model.
Machine-readable authority binding model for governance corpus resolver and constraint-pack generation.

authoritative_semantics: The issue-specific authority pack MUST be delivered as `instructions_<ISSUE>_v<N>.zip`. More generally, any non-patch issue-scoped downloadable zip emitted by chats MUST use `<artifact_kind>_<ISSUE>_v<N>.zip`, where `<artifact_kind>` is the first basename token and identifies payload kind. The patch basename contract `issue_<ISSUE>_v<N>.zip` MUST remain unchanged. The issue-specific authority pack MUST contain target scope, spec fingerprint, active bindings, active rule ids, full rule text, match basis, authoritative sources, shared contracts, downstream consumers, exception refs, required wiring, forbidden strategies, required validation, verification mode, verification method, oracle refs, and aggregate scope metadata. hash_pack.txt MUST be exactly one lowercase ASCII hex SHA-256 line over raw constraint_pack.json bytes.

Discovery resolver MUST fail closed on ambiguous symbol, duplicate semantics without allowed dedup, conflicting obligations, missing binding for authority-driven scope, missing verification contract, and pack recompute mismatch.
Final resolver MUST recompute the active authority pack from the authoritative corpus and MUST STOP on any mismatch, ambiguity, conflicting obligation set, or missing verification contract.

description: Constraint pack oracle. The implementation bridge artifact MUST carry a recomputable pack and a one-line lowercase SHA-256 hash over raw constraint_pack.json bytes.
Discovery resolver oracle. Ambiguous symbol, duplicate semantics, conflicting obligations, missing binding, and missing verification contract MUST stop the workflow.
Final resolver oracle. Pack recompute mismatch, ambiguity, conflicting obligations, and missing verification contract MUST stop the workflow.

statement: Whenever one component invokes another component (a "call boundary"), failures MUST NOT be silently swallowed.

heading_path: AudioMason2 Project Contract (PC) > 4. Non-Negotiable Architectural Invariants > 4.5 Mandatory Runtime Diagnostic Emission
statement:
If an invoked component fails, the invoking component MUST emit a diagnostic failure event via the authoritative entry point that preserves enough context to identify:
  - which component or operation failed,
  - which boundary invocation failed,
  - and the failure reason (at minimum: error type and message; ideally with causal context).

heading_path: AudioMason2 Project Contract (PC) > 4. Non-Negotiable Architectural Invariants > 4.5 Mandatory Runtime Diagnostic Emission
statement: Each component that performs work or participates in a call boundary MUST emit at least a start and an end state (succeeded/failed) for that work/boundary via the authoritative entry point.
Logging, exceptions, or return values do not substitute the mandatory emission to the authoritative diagnostic entry point.
heading_path: AudioMason2 Project Contract (PC) > 4. Non-Negotiable Architectural Invariants > 4.5 Mandatory Runtime Diagnostic Emission

statement: If you are looking for a bug, I expect you to find the root cause of the bug together with all consequences that it causes. The user will then upload the script output to the chat and the chat must automatically inspect it and continue in compliance with the project-chat instructions (PM).

I want to know whether the patch solves all problems fully, with quality, and correctly, and whether it creates no new ones.

statement: The handoff MUST contain a PLAN FREEZE section that rewrites the approved plan into numbered items P1..Pn without changing the number of items, their boundaries, scope, or meaning.
